mnemonics.title: PEMDAS for Operator Precedence
mnemonics.content: PEMDAS: Parentheses → Exponents → Multiplication/Division → Addition/Subtraction. Remember: Please Excuse My Dear Aunt Sally!

mnemonics.title: Variable Naming Rules
mnemonics.content: VOWELS rule: Variable names can have letters, underscores, numbers (not at start). Think: Valid Operator Within Unique Naming System

mnemonics.title: Python Data Types
mnemonics.content: I Float String Bool: I for Integer, Float for decimals, String for text, Bool for True/False. Remember: Indian Soldiers Fight Brave Battles

acronyms.usage: PEP 8 is the style guide, PEP 257 covers docstrings
Built-in Python IDE comes with standard installation
Interactive Python shell that reads, evaluates, and prints results
Executes bytecode compiled from Python source code

visualTips.visualization: Think of indentation like stairs - each level represents being 'inside' the previous level (like being inside a building, floor by floor)
Variables are like labeled boxes: the label is the variable name, the contents are the value. The box can hold different items (types) over time.
Think of strings as train cars - + joins them together, print() displays the whole train, sep acts as the coupling between cars

analogies.analogy: Like a human translator at a meeting - reads your English (Python code), converts it instantly to actions the computer understands.
Like a printer in an office - you send it something (text/data), and it produces output on paper (screen).
Like a survey form - it pauses and waits for the user to fill in their response before continuing.
Like containers in a kitchen - int is a whole egg carton, float is a measuring cup (partial amounts), str is a labeled jar, bool is a light switch (on/off).
